frameworks.name: decision uncertainty
frameworks.one_line: computational signature of confidence in perceptual choices guides adjustments

frameworks.name: pupil-linked arousal
frameworks.one_line: pupil diameter as proxy for central arousal modulating brain state

frameworks.name: serial choice bias
frameworks.one_line: trial-to-trial correlation structure of choices shaped by arousal

core_question: Does decision uncertainty drive pupil-linked arousal, and does that arousal in turn alter serial choice bias on subsequent trials?
key_assumptions: pupil diameter indexes central arousal state
decision uncertainty has identifiable computational signatures
arousal can shape ongoing choice behavior across trials
manipulations: post-choice pre-feedback interval for pupil measurement
key_findings: post-choice pupil dilation reflects three hallmark signatures of decision uncertainty
increased pupil-linked arousal boosts tendency to alternate choice on the next trial
interpretations: decision uncertainty rapidly drives pupil-linked arousal
arousal state shapes serial correlation structure of ongoing choice behavior
